comments.body: You need to sign your commit, and add a tests or [NO NEW TEST NEEDED] flag.

@rhatdan I tried to sign my commit. The DCO test does not approve.

I ran `gpg --gen-key` since I didn't have a key yet, and then signed my commit using `-S`.

```
$ git log --show-signature -1
commit 1d98a36ea6b4ea29cccbe057099e959cfc439de1 (HEAD -> ImageCreate_200_schema, origin/ImageCreate_200_schema)
gpg: Signature made Mon 07 Feb 2022 10:49:02 AM CET
gpg:                using EDDSA key 23FDF28B72B23CF4C11C0B57E0DE26F32851AEE4
gpg:                issuer "tom.deseyn@gmail.com"
gpg: Good signature from "Tom Deseyn <tom.deseyn@gmail.com>" [ultimate]
Author: Tom Deseyn <tom.deseyn@gmail.com>
Date:   Mon Feb 7 10:49:02 2022 +0100

    Add schema for ImageCreate 200 response.
```

What is missing?

> and add a tests or [NO NEW TEST NEEDED] flag.

@jwhonce I've added this flag. If this is not ok, let me know where I should add a test.
@tmds You do not have to sign your commits via gpg. You just have to add the Sign-off-by line to your commit message, see https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs
I've added `[NO NEW TEST NEEDED]` to the title of this PR. From CI failure, it looks like the wrong place. Where should I add this?
@tmds `[NO NEW TEST NEEDED]` is also needed somewhere in your commit message.
It should be `NO NEW TESTS` (plural) not `NO NEW TEST` but in you case because you just edit docs it is better to use `CI:DOCS` in the PR title so that unnecessary tests are skipped.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99*, *tmds* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
